name: Published PDE5 Class Transfer And Agent Directness Framework Baseline
summary: Stages a targeted baseline framework for deciding when sildenafil, vardenafil, avanafil, mixed PDE5-inhibitor, or class-level evidence may inform tadalafil claims and when it must remain adjacent. The bundle adds a search protocol, six new sources, three artifacts, four findings, and one bounded framework claim.
appraisal_follow_up_actions: endpoint_validity: No correction required; keep outcome-specific endpoints in separate review-question baselines.
endpoint_validity: No correction required; preserve this boundary in public copy.
exposure_directness: No correction required; apply the same metadata discipline to future sildenafil, vardenafil, avanafil, and mixed-PDE5 records.
exposure_directness: Keep exposure qualifiers attached to every future transfer statement.
population_directness: Keep directness language explicit in public synthesis and future outcome-specific baselines.
population_directness: Use this framework as the default, then refine directness during each outcome-specific baseline.
safety_risk_framing: No correction required; keep timing and product-label language agent-specific.
safety_risk_framing: No correction required; retain hard-stop wording near any PDE5 benefit discussion.
source_fidelity: No correction required; re-check label dates during future safety or transfer updates.
source_fidelity: Keep the full-text follow-up actions open before increasing confidence in detailed review-derived transfer rules.
synthesis_overreach: No correction required; use this claim as a gate for future adjacent PDE5 syntheses.
synthesis_overreach: No correction required; maintain this posture in public summaries.
change_note: Creates the first submitted framework for PDE5 class-transfer and agent-directness decisions.
